summary: Backend-only validation of the safe Vibe Stakes / Dynamic Premium Pricing iteration against the preview URL. All 33 regression tests + 6 new TestSafeVibeStakes tests + 10 extra hardening tests = 49/49 passed. Treasury PII anonymization, 4/3/3 rake math (round + decimal amounts), grandfathering, surge state, admin gating, and price-curve identity (current + step == next) all verified end-to-end via real HTTP.

minor.issue: Reserve `balance_usd` accumulates float-rounding artifacts (observed 25.790999999999997). Each subscribe rounds component cuts to 4 decimals but $inc on the reserve doesn't snap the running total. Cosmetic — admins reading dashboards will see ugly trailing 9s. Consider Decimal or final round on read.
Anon ID format uses `…` (U+2026) which is fine for browsers but escapes oddly in raw JSON tooling. Not a bug, just a nit.

No idempotency on `payment_ref` — repeated POSTs with the same ref will run the rake multiple times, double-counting reserve and pool boost. Stripe webhooks retry; recommend a unique index on `gvdsg_operating_ledger.ref` and a 200/no-op short-circuit when the ref already exists.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Add idempotency guard on /api/premium/subscribe keyed off payment_ref to survive Stripe webhook retries (medium-priority hardening before going live).
OPTIONAL: Round stability_reserve.balance_usd to 2 decimals on read (or store as cents int) — purely cosmetic.
Nothing blocking ship — all assertions in the review request passed.
critical_code_review_comments: profit_share.py is 572 lines — fine but approaching the 700-line split threshold; consider extracting the scheduler + payout engine into a sub-module on next change.

premium_pricing.py /subscribe is NOT idempotent on payment_ref. Stripe ALWAYS retries on 5xx and sometimes on transient network blips. Without a unique index or pre-flight check, the same successful payment can credit the reserve, the pool boost, AND the +200 stake grant twice. Highest-priority hardening item.

_pool_snapshot() uses DEMO_QUARTERLY_PROFIT_USD as the headline number — the pool reported in /pool and /treasury currently does NOT include the running pool_boost from /profit_share_pool_boosts. Once Stripe revenue is plumbed, the boost collection sums must be added in or the dashboard understates the pool.

Treasury anonymization slices first 6 + last 2 chars. For short user_ids like `TEST_d` plus suffix, this can still leak the full id (we observed `TEST_d…62` for `TEST_driver_xx62`). Acceptable for a public dashboard but flag if any user_id format is < 16 chars.

_maybe_trigger_surge runs on every accrual and does a count_documents over the last hour — at scale this is an O(N) scan. Add an index on profit_share_accruals.at OR cache the trigger window in Redis.
Surge expiry uses string ISO comparison via fromisoformat — works but Mongo TTL index on a real datetime field would be more robust and self-cleaning.

Pydantic SubscribePayload accepts payment_amount_usd ge=0.01 but no upper bound — a malicious authed user could POST $1B and pollute the reserve. Add le=10000 (or whatever max plan tier) plus tier validation.

process_premium_subscription doesn't verify the payment_ref actually corresponds to a real Stripe charge — if this endpoint is reachable directly (it is, /api/premium/subscribe), any authed user can self-grant Premium for free. Should be webhook-only or require a server-signed token from the payment processor.
test_credentials: POST /api/auth/demo-login (no params, returns bearer token + user_id)
seed_data_creation: Two real subscriptions were created during testing via /api/premium/subscribe ($10.00 ref=pytest_rake_*, $12.99 ref=pytest_extra_*). These bumped gvdsg_reserve.balance_usd by ~$6.90 and added two pool-boost rows. Test-only refs are prefixed `pytest_`.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Endpoints all live and contract-stable on https://social-connect-953.preview.emergentagent.com. The combined regression+hardening pytest suite is the canonical reference: `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iter_global_vibez_dsg_2026_01*.py`. Next iteration MUST address the /premium/subscribe idempotency + payment-verification gaps before shipping Stripe — flagged in critical_code_review_comments.
rca of the issue: No bugs to RCA — all features in the review request pass contract + math + auth checks. The three minor items above are hardening recommendations, not regressions.
